name: Florida
notes_normalized: Cannabis is illegal in Florida for recreational use under state law, but medical cannabis is legal under a regulated state program. Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States. Possession is limited to Medical only.
notes_raw: style="background:#80CE69;" | {{Hs|3}} Cannabis in Florida is illegal for recreational use. (October 18, 1933) An October 20, 1933, editor. The editorial writer called for the prohibition of marijuana:. it may or may not be wholly true that the pernicious marijuana cigarette is responsible for the murderous mania of a Tampa young man in exterminating all the members of his family within his reach — but whether or not the poisonous mind-wrecking weed is mainly accountable for the tragedy its sale should not be and should never have been permitted here. In 2013, supporters of a constitutional amendment to legalize medical cannabis began collecting signature to place the issue on the 2014 ballot. House Bill 307 was signed into law by Governor Scott on March 25, 2016, to expand the state's Right to Try Act to allow terminally ill patients to use cannabis. The bill also sought to address problems that had arisen with the implementation of the state's CBD law that was approved in 2014. In 2016, a second attempt was made to pass a constitutional amendment to legalize medical cannabis in Florida. Possession of up to is a misdemeanor offense, punishable by up to a year in jail, a fine of up to $1000, and the suspension of one's driver's license. Several cities and counties have enacted reforms to apply lesser penalties, however. Persons issued a citation are required to pay a $100 fine or perform two days of community service. The penalty was set at $100 for a first offense, $250 for a second, and $500 for a third. First and second offenses are punishable by a $100 fine; a third offense cannot be cited. Instead of a fine, cited individuals can opt for 10 hours of community service. The penalty was set at $75 for a first offense, $150 for a second, and $300 for a third.
